Caption: Scheme of the battery system considered in this study. Notice that, despite in practice there are also Electrolyte particles inside the Cathode Zone, in this study the whole Cathode Zone will be considered composed of a pseudo-homogeneous material with properties described in Table 3 (Hypothesis 6).

(a) Comparison between simulated results with parameters from literature (dashed line) vs. simulated results with estimated parameters (continuous line) vs. experimental results presented by Raijmakers et al. [10] (dots). (b) Parity Plot between simulated (with estimated parameters) and experimental results. The inputs used in the simulations are the ones displayed in Table 3. Average RMSD = 0.06 V for the optimized parameters and 0.11 V for the parameters from the literature.

Caption: Concentration profile in the Cathode Zone and Overpotential Profile for (a–c) Case 5.0, (d–f) Case 1.0, (g–i) Case 0.2. All the simulation parameters were the ones stated in Table 3 except for the maximum ion concentration in the Cathode Zone ( a M ⊕ max ) were multiplied by a factor of 5, 1 and 0.2 respectively. Simulations were generated for C-Rate = − 1.

Simultaneous variation analysis of maximum ion concentration ( a M ⊕ max ) in the Cathode Zone and initial concentration of vacancy sites ( a 0 ) in the Electrolyte Zone. The standard values of such parameters (cf. Table 3) were multiplied by a factor ranging from 0.1 to 10. The Nernst Curve is also displayed in a dashed curve. Simulations were generated for C-Rate = − 1.

Overpotential Profile for (a) Case 0.1, (b) Case 1, (c) Case 10. All the simulation parameters were the ones stated in Table 3 except for the maximum ion concentration ( a M ⊕ max ) in the Cathode Zone and initial concentration of vacancy sites ( a 0 ) in the Electrolyte Zone that were multiplied by a factor of [0.1; 1.0; 10.0] respectively. Simulations were generated for C-Rate = − 1.

Simultaneous variation analysis of all the parameters studied in this work ( D M ⊕ , D e − , D M + , D n − , k 1 , k 2 , a M ⊕ max , a 0 ), that were multiplied by a factor ranging from 0.5 to 500. All the other parameters were kept constant as in Table 3. The Nernst Curve is also displayed in a dashed curve. As discussed in the Concentration Section, to keep the absolute current constant, simulations were generated for C-Rate = [-2.000; −1.333; −1.000; −0.666; −0.500; −0.200; −0.100; −0.050; −0.010; −0.002] for Cases [0.5; 0.75; 1; 1.5; 2; 5; 10; 20; 100; 500] respectively.
